description:
You are a deployment engineer who has just finished pushing three successive
    application releases into the staging area located under
    /home/user/deployment/releases/.  
    Every release directory (e.g. /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.0.0/)
    already contains a markdown file named NOTES.md that enumerates every
    user-visible change in that release.

    Your goal is to generate two artefacts and perform one configuration edit
    so that the deployment pipeline can promote the latest build to production.

    1. Create a release-wide changelog file at
       /home/user/deployment/changelog.log using *exactly* the following
       fixed-width, pipe-separated layout (the automated tests will check exact
       spacing, capitalisation and number formatting):

          Release | Features | Fixes
          vX.Y.Z  | <N>      | <M>

       a) The very first line must be the literal header shown above
          (single spaces around the pipes, capital “R” in “Release”).
       b) One data line per release must follow, sorted in ascending semantic-
          version order (v1.0.0 first, v1.1.0 second, and so on).
       c) Count how many lines inside each NOTES.md start with the exact prefix
          “FEATURE:” (features) or “FIX:” (fixes) to populate <N> and <M>.
       d) The spacing must match the header column widths:
             • “Release” column 8 characters wide (left-aligned)
             • “Features” column 9 characters wide (right-aligned)
             • “Fixes” column 6 characters wide (right-aligned)
          Use plain spaces, no tabs.

       Example of a single, correctly aligned data line  
       (DO **NOT** copy; generate real numbers):
          v1.0.0 |         2 |     1

    2. Append a one-line audit entry to /home/user/deployment/logs/update_actions.log
       (create intermediate directories if they do not exist).  
       The line must follow this exact pattern (single spaces only):

          [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM] generated changelog for X releases

       where the timestamp is the current system time **in UTC** when the line
       is written, and X is the count of releases processed (should be “3” given
       the current staging contents).

    3. Edit /home/user/deployment/config/app.conf so that the property

          version=

       is updated (or added if missing) to contain the latest semantic version
       discovered during step 1 (e.g. “version=1.2.0”).  Leave every other line
       of the file unchanged and preserve its original order.

    Verification notes for you:
    • All three paths must exist when you are done:
        /home/user/deployment/changelog.log
        /home/user/deployment/logs/update_actions.log
        /home/user/deployment/config/app.conf
    • The automated grader will perform byte-level comparison of
      /home/user/deployment/changelog.log and a regex match for the audit line,
      so follow the instructed spacing and punctuation exactly.
    • Do not hard-code any numbers; derive counts programmatically from the
      NOTES.md files.
truth:
Initial file system snapshot before the agent starts:

    /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.0.0/NOTES.md
        FEATURE: Add login functionality
        FEATURE: Add logout functionality
        FIX: Correct typo in README

    /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.1.0/NOTES.md
        FEATURE: Add password reset

    /home/user/deployment/releases/v1.2.0/NOTES.md
        FEATURE: Improve UI
        FIX: Fix crash on launch
        FEATURE: Add dark mode
        FIX: Memory leak fix
        FEATURE: Optimize database queries

    /home/user/deployment/config/app.conf
        # Application configuration
        environment=staging
        # the version key will be added/updated by the agent

    Final state expected after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/deployment/changelog.log (exact bytes)
          Release | Features | Fixes
          v1.0.0  |         2 |     1
          v1.1.0  |         1 |     0
          v1.2.0  |         3 |     2

       • Header line length is 24 bytes including newline.
       • Every data line is 24 bytes including newline (LF only).

    2. /home/user/deployment/logs/update_actions.log  
       must contain **at least one** line that matches the regex  
       ^\[20\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}\] generated changelog for 3 releases$

    3. /home/user/deployment/config/app.conf must end up containing the line
          version=1.2.0
       appearing exactly once (either appended or replacing an older value).

    No other files should be modified or created.
